Guideline: Return HTML fragments, not JSON
Description: htmx swaps server responses directly into the DOM, so endpoints should render HTML partials rather than data payloads.
Do: Respond to hx-* requests with the HTML fragment to be swapped in.
Don't: Return JSON and rebuild the DOM with client-side JavaScript.

Guideline: Point hx-target at the element to swap
Description: hx-target directs the response into a specific element instead of the triggering element's innerHTML.
Do: Use a CSS selector or extended selector (this, closest, next, find) for hx-target.
Don't: Pepper the DOM with id attributes just to route every swap.

Guideline: Prefer relative extended selectors over id soup
Description: Extended selectors like closest and next resolve targets relative to the element, avoiding scattered ids.
Do: Use hx-target="closest tr" or next to locate the target contextually.
Don't: Add a unique id to every row or cell only to serve as a swap target.

Guideline: Choose the swap style that matches the update
Description: hx-swap controls how content is placed relative to the target (innerHTML, outerHTML, beforeend, etc.).
Do: Use outerHTML to replace the whole element, beforeend to append.
Don't: Assume the default innerHTML fits every case, replacing content you meant to keep.
Code Bad: Replace me

Guideline: Time swaps to your CSS transitions
Description: The swap:<time> and settle:<time> modifiers delay the swap/settle so animations can complete.
Do: Add swap:1s to hold the old content while a fade-out transition runs.
Don't: Trigger CSS transitions but let htmx remove the element instantly.

Guideline: Keep element ids stable for CSS transitions
Description: htmx only animates between old and new content when the element's id is preserved across swaps.
Do: Reuse the same id on the swapped element so htmx can settle transitions.
Don't: Change or drop the id on each response and expect transitions to work.

Guideline: Debounce as-you-type requests with delay
Description: The delay modifier resets on each event, so requests only fire after the user pauses.
Do: Use keyup changed delay:500ms for active search inputs.
Don't: Fire a request on every keystroke with no delay.
Code Good: <input name="q" hx-get="/search" hx-trigger="keyup changed delay:500ms" hx-target="#results">

Guideline: Use changed to skip no-op requests
Description: The changed modifier only issues a request when the element's value actually changed.
Do: Combine changed with keyup so arrow keys and modifiers don't fire requests.
Don't: Send a request even when the input value is identical.

Guideline: Throttle high-frequency events
Description: throttle ignores repeat events until the interval passes, unlike delay which resets the timer.
Do: Use throttle:500ms for rapid events where you want a steady request cadence.
Don't: Use delay for a firehose event expecting it to rate-limit like throttle.

Guideline: Listen for events from other elements with from:
Description: The from:<selector> modifier lets an element react to events fired elsewhere in the DOM.
Do: Use from:body for global keyboard shortcuts or HX-Trigger header events.
Don't: Wrap unrelated elements just to co-locate a listener with its source.

Guideline: Add from:body to HX-Trigger header events
Description: Response-header-fired events dispatch in a different DOM hierarchy, so listeners need from:body.
Do: Declare hx-trigger="my-event from:body" when firing via the HX-Trigger header.
Don't: Listen for an HX-Trigger header event on the local element only.

Guideline: Update other regions with hx-swap-oob
Description: Out-of-band swaps match by id to update elements outside the main target in a single response.
Do: Mark extra fragments hx-swap-oob="true" to piggy-back updates on one request.
Don't: Fire separate requests for each region that changes together.
Code Bad: Saved!

Guideline: Wrap OOB table rows in a template
Description: Elements like tr/td can't stand alone during parsing, so OOB fragments must be wrapped in a template tag.
Do: Enclose an OOB <tr> in <template> so the browser parses it correctly.
Don't: Return a bare OOB <tr> and expect it to swap reliably.

Guideline: Progressively enhance links with hx-boost
Description: hx-boost turns anchors and forms into AJAX requests while still working without JavaScript.
Do: Wrap navigation in hx-boost="true" so it degrades gracefully.
Don't: Replace plain links with JS handlers that break when scripting is off.

Guideline: Detect htmx requests via HX-Request
Description: Boosted and htmx requests send the HX-Request header so servers can return partials vs full pages.
Do: Check HX-Request server-side to decide between a fragment and a full document.
Don't: Return the same full HTML page to both boosted and direct requests.
Code Good: <!-- server: if HX-Request header present -> return fragment -->
Code Bad: <!-- server: always return full <html> page for boosted nav -->

Guideline: Set multipart encoding for file uploads
Description: File uploads require hx-encoding="multipart/form-data" on the form.
Do: Add hx-encoding="multipart/form-data" to any form with a file input.
Don't: Submit a file input with the default urlencoded encoding.

Guideline: Return 422 to keep validation errors in the form
Description: htmx ignores 4xx bodies by default, but you can configure 422 responses to swap validation error markup.
Do: Respond 422 with the re-rendered form so inline errors are shown.
Don't: Return a 200 for validation failures, muddling success and error states.
Code Good: <input name="email" hx-post="/validate" hx-trigger="changed delay:500ms" hx-target="closest div" hx-swap="outerHTML">

Guideline: Synchronize form field requests with hx-sync
Description: hx-sync coordinates in-flight requests between elements to avoid race conditions on a shared form.
Do: Use hx-sync="closest form:abort" so field validation cancels on submit.
Don't: Let field validation and form submit race each other unmanaged.

Guideline: Always re-validate on the server
Description: Client-side validation can always be bypassed, so server-side checks are mandatory.
Do: Treat client validation as UX only and enforce all rules on the server.
Don't: Rely solely on browser or client htmx validation for correctness.
Code Good: <!-- server rejects invalid input regardless of client checks -->
Code Bad: <!-- server trusts client-validated input without re-checking -->

Guideline: Show a loading indicator with hx-indicator
Description: htmx adds the htmx-request class during a request, revealing elements marked htmx-indicator.
Do: Reference a spinner via hx-indicator so users see request progress.
Don't: Leave requests with no visible feedback during network latency.

Guideline: Disable controls in flight with hx-disabled-elt
Description: hx-disabled-elt adds the disabled attribute to elements while a request is active, preventing double-submits.
Do: Set hx-disabled-elt="this" on buttons that trigger mutations.
Don't: Allow users to click a submit button repeatedly during a slow request.

Guideline: Confirm destructive actions
Description: hx-confirm shows a native confirmation dialog before issuing the request.
Do: Add hx-confirm to delete and other irreversible actions.
Don't: Delete records on a single unconfirmed click.

Guideline: Bound polling and let the server stop it
Description: Fixed polling uses every <time>; respond with HTTP 286 to tell htmx to stop polling.
Do: Return 286 once a job completes so the poll loop ends.
Don't: Poll a completed resource forever with no stop condition.
Code Good: ...

Guideline: Load below-the-fold content lazily
Description: The load and revealed triggers defer fetching content until needed.
Do: Use hx-trigger="revealed" for infinite scroll and load for deferred panels.
Don't: Render every expensive panel eagerly on first page load.

Guideline: Vary cached responses on HX-Request
Description: Partials and full pages share URLs, so use Vary: HX-Request to keep caches from colliding.
Do: Send Vary: HX-Request (or a cache-buster) when a URL serves both fragment and full page.
Don't: Cache a fragment and a full page under the same key.
Code Bad: <!-- server: no Vary header; fragment served as full page from cache -->

Guideline: Return 204 to swap nothing
Description: A 204 No Content response tells htmx to perform no swap, useful for fire-and-forget actions.
Do: Return 204 when an action should not change the DOM.
Don't: Return empty 200 markup that wipes the target unintentionally.

Guideline: Update history only for real URLs
Description: hx-push-url adds history entries, but every pushed URL must return a full page on direct navigation.
Do: Push URLs that your server can fully render on reload/deep-link.
Don't: Push a URL that only responds correctly to an htmx fragment request.

Guideline: Return HTML instead of 3xx redirects
Description: htmx-specific response headers aren't processed on 3xx redirects; return the fragment or use HX-Redirect.
Do: Directly return the new HTML fragment, skipping the Post/Redirect/Get dance.
Don't: Issue a 302 redirect and expect HX-Push-Url style headers to apply.
Code Good: <!-- server: 200 with new fragment, or HX-Redirect header -->

Guideline: Only call routes you control
Description: htmx injects a route's response into the page, so calling untrusted domains risks script injection.
Do: Use relative URLs; rely on htmx 2 disabling cross-domain requests by default.
Don't: Point hx-get at an external domain you do not control.

Guideline: Always use an auto-escaping template engine
Description: Escaping all dynamic content in an auto-escaping engine is the core defense against XSS.
Do: Render user content through an auto-escaping template so markup is neutralized.
Don't: Concatenate user input into HTML without escaping.

Guideline: Only serve user content inside HTML tag bodies
Description: Escaping does not protect dangerous contexts like script/style bodies, tag names, or attributes.
Do: Place user data as text between tags, never as a tag name or attribute source.
Don't: Interpolate user input into a <script>, <style>, tag name, or attribute.

Guideline: Add CSRF tokens via hx-headers
Description: CSRF tokens can be attached to all htmx requests by setting hx-headers on a root element.
Do: Set hx-headers with the CSRF token on html/body so it inherits to requests.
Don't: Send state-changing hx-post requests without any CSRF token.

Guideline: Secure auth cookies with SameSite=Lax
Description: Cookies set Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite=Lax defend against XSS token theft and CSRF.
Do: Set Secure; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax on auth cookies.
Don't: Store auth tokens in localStorage and attach them via JavaScript.
Code Bad: <!-- token kept in localStorage, read by JS -->

Guideline: Scrub or disable htmx on injected raw HTML
Description: Injecting third-party HTML is discouraged; hx-disable stops htmx processing on untrusted subtrees.
Do: Wrap untrusted content in hx-disable and strip hx-/script attributes.
Don't: Inject raw third-party HTML and let htmx process its attributes.

Guideline: Keep sensitive pages out of history cache
Description: hx-history="false" prevents a page snapshot being saved to localStorage history.
Do: Set hx-history="false" on pages showing sensitive data.
Don't: Let pages with private data be snapshotted into the history cache.

Guideline: Hoist shared attributes via inheritance
Description: Most hx-* attributes inherit to children, so common config can be declared once on a parent.
Do: Place shared hx-target or hx-headers on a parent to avoid repetition.
Don't: Repeat identical hx-* attributes on every child element.

Guideline: Keep behavior local and hypermedia-friendly
Description: Locality of Behaviour favors scripting that complements hypermedia, using inline hx-on for element events.
Do: Handle element events with hx-on and dispatch events for component talk.
Don't: Move all behavior into distant JS files that rebuild client state.
Code Bad: // large external controller manipulating the DOM out of view

Guideline: Reprocess dynamically injected htmx markup
Description: When JavaScript inserts htmx-attributed HTML, htmx.process() must be called to activate it.
Do: Call htmx.process(el) after injecting htmx markup via JS.
Don't: Inject htmx attributes with JS and expect them to work untouched.
Code Bad: <!-- js: el.innerHTML = html; // attributes never activated -->
